title: X-Loopctl-Last-Known-STH Header Validation and Halt on Divergence

story.as_a: tenant operator needing cryptographic tamper evidence on the audit chain
story.i_want_to: have every API call cross-check the caller's local STH cache against the server's current STH, and halt the tenant's custody operations if a divergence is detected
story.so_that: an attacker rewriting the chain on the server side must simultaneously rewrite every connected agent's memory — an infeasible requirement

rationale: US-26.5.1 ships the subscription and cache. This story adds the enforcement: request-time divergence detection plus halt on mismatch. Without this story, the cache is informational but unused.

acceptance_criteria.description: mcp-server attaches `X-Loopctl-Last-Known-STH: <position>:<base64url_sig_prefix_16_bytes>` header to every outbound request if the session cache has a valid STH.

Loopctl plug `Loopctl.Web.Plugs.ValidateWitnessHeader` runs on every authenticated request and checks: (a) header is present (optional during 60-day soft launch, required after), (b) position ≤ server's current STH position for the tenant, (c) signature prefix matches the loopctl-side record for that position.

If the signature prefix doesn't match the known STH at that position → divergence detected. The response returns 409 with error code `witness_divergence` and remediation pointing at `/wiki/witness-protocol`.

On divergence, the tenant's custody operations are halted: `tenants.custody_halted_at` is set, all subsequent custody-critical endpoints return 503 with error code `tenant_halted` until an operator clears the halt via break-glass.
Halt event is logged in the audit chain with action `witness_divergence_detected` and payload including the agent's claimed STH, the server's STH, and the position where they diverge.
Operator alert webhook fires on halt (event type `tenant.custody_halted`). The webhook payload includes the divergence details.
`POST /api/v1/admin/tenants/:id/clear-halt` requires a fresh WebAuthn assertion from a root authenticator. Clears the halt and writes a `halt_cleared` audit entry with the operator's signature.
Dashboard at `/admin/halts` shows active halts across tenants (operator-scoped) with diagnostic details and a clear-halt button.

The witness-header requirement is routed through enforcement flag `:witness_header_missing`. In shadow mode the plug logs every request missing the header (or carrying a stale one) but allows it through. When the operator flips the flag to `:strict`, the plug rejects the request with 412 Precondition Required and a remediation pointing at `/wiki/witness-protocol`. The shadow mode branch and the strict branch use the same plug — only the flag determines whether a violation results in reject.
OpenAPI spec updates for the new error codes and the halt/clear-halt endpoints. CHANGELOG entry.

test_cases.name: Valid STH header passes
steps: Make an authenticated request with the correct header

test_cases.name: Invalid signature prefix triggers divergence halt
steps: Send request with a forged/wrong signature prefix at a real position

test_cases.name: Subsequent requests after halt return 503 tenant_halted
Clear halt with WebAuthn restores operations
steps: POST /admin/tenants/:id/clear-halt with a valid WebAuthn assertion

technical_notes: Plug location: lib/loopctl_web/plugs/validate_witness_header.ex, mounted in the :api pipeline.
Signature prefix comparison: compare the first 16 bytes of the STH signature (64 bytes total). Prefix is enough for divergence detection and keeps the header short.
Halt state is stored on the tenants table. All custody endpoints check this at the plug level.
Clear-halt endpoint is separate from the normal admin API to require WebAuthn.

Enforcement flag: :witness_header_missing starts in :shadow when Phase 5 ships. Operator observes the dashboard for 7 days, confirms all active agents have been updated, then flips to :strict via the admin endpoint.
Reference: docs/chain-of-custody-v2.md section 4.4 and section 2.7 (halt on byzantine).
